key_decisions.decision: Always eagerly evaluate mw(next) at handler construction time
key_decisions.reasoning: The simplest correct fix; mw(next) is idempotent for any well-behaved middleware, so pre-building once is always safe.

solve_notes: Moved the mw(next) call from inside the per-request handler closure to the outer initialization closure, storing the result in withMW. The inner closure now calls withMW.ServeHTTP when maybeFn(r) is true, eliminating the per-request middleware reconstruction.
task_text:
Fix Maybe middleware reconstructing middleware chain on every request

The `Maybe` middleware in `middleware/maybe.go` calls `mw(next)` inside
the per-request handler closure each time `maybeFn(r)` returns true.
This rebuilds the entire wrapped middleware chain on every qualifying
request instead of once at initialization time.
tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just the edited function. No thrash-preventing defs are needed — the file is 18 lines, the function is self-contained, and no external context is needed.
